wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Ark Agentic project overview section focused on introducing the lightweight ReAct agent framework. Explain its purpose as a business-focused AI agent development platform, core architecture principles, and relationship to the broader AI agent ecosystem. Document the framework's key capabilities including multi-domain agent support (insurance and securities), ReAct decision loop implementation, A2UI system for rich user interfaces, and plugin-based extensibility. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners new to AI agents and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'ReAct loop', 'A2UI blocks', 'agent runtime', and 'plugin system'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the framework's value proposition and common use cases. Document the project's position in the AI agent landscape and its unique differentiators.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bootstrap System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Bootstrap system focused on framework initialization and lifecycle orchestration. Explain the stateful orchestrator pattern that drives lifecycle components through init/start/stop phases. Document the automatic loading of framework defaults including AgentsLifecycle and TracingLifecycle, and how plugins are integrated between these core components. Detail the HTTP-agnostic design principle and the lazy import strategy to prevent circular dependencies. Include practical examples of bootstrap configuration, component registration order, and context sharing between components. Document the idempotent initialization process, reverse-order shutdown mechanism, and error handling during teardown. Provide guidance on extending the bootstrap system and integrating custom lifecycle components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Storage Mode Selection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the storage mode selection system in Ark Agentic. Explain how the DB_TYPE environment variable determines the active storage backend (file or sqlite), the default fallback behavior, and validation logic. Document the StorageMode type definition and current() function that resolves the active mode. Include practical examples of environment configuration for different deployment scenarios. Address the is_database() utility function and its role in determining SQL engine availability. Provide troubleshooting guidance for unsupported storage modes and common configuration issues. Explain the historical context of the DB_TYPE naming convention and semantic evolution from database selection to storage mode selection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Ark Agentic framework focused on helping new users quickly understand and begin using the framework. Explain the two main user paths: business application developers who want to build agents, and framework developers who maintain the core. Document the complete installation process including CLI installation via uv tool install or pip, project initialization using ark-agentic init command, and environment configuration with .env files. Provide step-by-step instructions for creating a first project, understanding the generated directory structure, and running both terminal and API modes. Include practical examples of basic agent configuration, tool creation, and initial testing. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for new users. Make the content accessible to developers new to AI agents while ensuring they understand the framework's capabilities and workflow.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Ark Agentic: Lightweight ReAct Agent Framework

## Project Introduction

Ark Agentic is a lightweight ReAct agent framework designed for business application development. The project provides both a powerful foundation for building intelligent agents and a comprehensive CLI toolchain for scaffolding production-ready projects.

### Purpose Statement
Ark Agentic aims to bridge the gap between AI research and real-world business applications by offering a pragmatic, extensible framework that allows developers to focus on business logic rather than infrastructure concerns.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Developer-Friendly**: Provide a simple, intuitive interface for creating business-focused agents
- **Production-Ready**: Include essential features like persistence, observability, and monitoring
- **Extensible Architecture**: Support multiple business domains through modular agent design
- **Observability-First**: Built-in tracing and monitoring capabilities out of the box

### Target Audience
- **Business Application Developers**: Teams building AI-powered business solutions
- **Framework Maintainers**: Contributors working on the core runtime and tooling
- **Enterprise Integrators**: Organizations deploying AI agents at scale

## Technical Architecture

The framework follows a clean, layered architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    subgraph "Business Layer"
        A[Agent Definitions]
        B[Business Tools]
        C[Custom Skills]
        D[Domain-Specific Logic]
    end
    
    subgraph "Core Runtime"
        E[AgentRunner]
        F[Session Manager]
        G[Memory Manager]
        H[Tool Registry]
    end
    
    subgraph "Infrastructure Layer"
        I[Storage Abstraction]
        J[Plugin System]
        K[Observability]
        L[Streaming Events]
    end
    
    subgraph "External Systems"
        M[LLM Providers]
        N[Database Engines]
        O[File Systems]
    end
    
    A --> E
    B --> H
    C --> E
    D --> E
    E --> F
    E --> G
    E --> H
    F --> I
    G --> I
    H --> I
    I --> N
    I --> O
    E --> M
    E --> K
    K --> L
```

### Component Breakdown

The architecture consists of several key layers:

**Core Runtime Layer**: Houses the fundamental agent execution engine, including the ReAct loop implementation, session management, and memory systems.

**Plugin System**: Provides optional functionality through a lifecycle-managed plugin architecture, enabling features like API endpoints, studio interfaces, and job scheduling.

**Storage Abstraction**: Implements a flexible persistence layer supporting both file-based and SQLite backends with a clean protocol-based design.

**Observability Layer**: Built-in support for multiple tracing providers (Console, Phoenix, Langfuse, OTLP) with automatic LangChain instrumentation.

### Design Patterns

The framework employs several proven design patterns:

- **Factory Pattern**: Used extensively for creating agents, tools, and repositories
- **Plugin Architecture**: Lifecycle-managed components with dependency injection
- **Repository Pattern**: Abstracted storage layer with multiple backends
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven streaming with callback mechanisms
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable LLM providers and storage engines

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

The framework provides multiple entry points depending on deployment needs:

**FastAPI Application Entry** (`src/ark_agentic/app.py`)
- Central application bootstrap with plugin orchestration
- HTTP API endpoint registration and middleware configuration
- Environment-based feature toggling

**CLI Tooling** (`src/ark_agentic/cli/main.py`)
- Project scaffolding with customizable templates
- Agent addition workflow for extending existing projects
- Version management and utility commands

**Agent Definition** (`src/ark_agentic/agents/insurance/agent.py`, `src/ark_agentic/agents/securities/agent.py`)
- Domain-specific agent configuration
- Tool registration and skill integration
- Customizable behavior through environment variables

### Core Modules

**Runtime Engine** (`src/ark_agentic/core/runtime/runner.py`)
- Implements the ReAct decision loop with configurable limits
- Manages tool execution and result processing
- Handles session state and conversation history

**Storage Abstraction** (`src/ark_agentic/core/storage/`)
- Protocol definitions for repositories
- Factory methods for backend selection
- File and SQLite implementation variants

**A2UI System** (`src/ark_agentic/core/a2ui/`)
- Agent-generated UI framework for rich interactions
- Block composition and rendering system
- Template-based UI generation

### Configuration Approach

The framework uses environment-based configuration with sensible defaults:

**Environment Variables** (`pyproject.toml`)
- LLM provider selection and authentication
- Feature toggles for optional components
- Storage backend configuration
- Observability settings

**Plugin Configuration** (`src/ark_agentic/plugins/`)
- Modular feature enablement through lifecycle components
- HTTP route registration and middleware setup
- Cross-plugin communication via AppContext

### External Dependencies

Key external libraries include:

- **LangChain**: LLM integration and chain orchestration
- **FastAPI**: HTTP API framework with async support
- **SQLAlchemy**: Database ORM and migration support
- **OpenTelemetry**: Distributed tracing and observability
- **Pydantic**: Data validation and serialization

### Integration Points

**LLM Provider Integration**
- Multi-provider support (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
- Automatic retry and fallback mechanisms
- Streaming response handling

**Storage Backend Selection**
- File-based persistence for development
- SQLite for production deployments
- Easy migration between backends

**Observability Integration**
- Multiple provider support (Phoenix, Langfuse, Console)
- Automatic LangChain instrumentation
- Custom span attributes and metadata

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    subgraph "Bootstrap Lifecycle"
        A[AgentsLifecycle]
        B[APIPlugin]
        C[NotificationsPlugin]
        D[JobsPlugin]
        E[StudioPlugin]
        F[TracingLifecycle]
    end
    
    subgraph "Core Runtime"
        G[AgentRunner]
        H[SessionManager]
        I[MemoryManager]
        J[ToolExecutor]
    end
    
    A --> G
    B --> G
    C --> G
    D --> G
    E --> G
    F --> G
    G --> H
    G --> I
    G --> J
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Domain Agent Support**
The framework includes built-in examples for insurance and securities domains, demonstrating how to implement business-specific agents with domain knowledge and specialized tools.

**Rich UI Generation**
Through the A2UI system, agents can generate interactive cards, forms, and dashboards that provide immediate visual feedback to users while maintaining conversational context.

**Flexible Persistence**
Storage abstraction supports both file-based development and SQLite production deployments, with easy migration paths between environments.

**Comprehensive Observability**
Built-in tracing with multiple provider support enables detailed monitoring of agent behavior, tool execution, and user interactions.

### Implementation Highlights

**Agent Creation Workflow** (`src/ark_agentic/agents/`)
- Standardized agent definition pattern
- Skill-based behavior extension
- Tool integration with automatic validation

**Plugin System** (`src/ark_agentic/plugins/`)
- Lifecycle-managed component architecture
- HTTP route registration and middleware
- Cross-plugin communication patterns

**Storage Abstraction** (`src/ark_agentic/core/storage/`)
- Protocol-based design for backend flexibility
- Factory pattern for repository instantiation
- Migration support between storage backends

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Initialization
    Initialization --> AgentCreation: Bootstrap Complete
    AgentCreation --> Running: Warmup Complete
    Running --> ToolExecution: Tool Call
    ToolExecution --> Running: Tool Result
    Running --> UIGeneration: A2UI Request
    UIGeneration --> Running: UI Rendered
    Running --> Streaming: SSE Response
    Streaming --> Running: Stream Complete
    Running --> [*]: Stop Signal
```

The framework's architecture ensures that each component has a clear responsibility while maintaining loose coupling through well-defined interfaces. This design enables easy extension, testing, and deployment across various environments and use cases.

**Sources:**
- [pyproject.toml](pyproject.toml)
- [app.py](src/ark_agentic/app.py)
- [bootstrap.py](src/ark_agentic/core/protocol/bootstrap.py)
- [agent.py](src/ark_agentic/agents/insurance/agent.py)
- [runner.py](src/ark_agentic/core/runtime/runner.py)
- [composer.py](src/ark_agentic/core/a2ui/composer.py)
- [plugin.py](src/ark_agentic/plugins/api/plugin.py)
- [main.py](src/ark_agentic/cli/main.py)
- [README.md](README.md)
- [architecture-c4.md](docs/architecture-c4.md)
- [database-and-storage-abstraction.md](docs/database-and-storage-abstraction.md)
- [blocks.py](src/ark_agentic/core/a2ui/blocks.py)
- [tracing.py](src/ark_agentic/core/observability/tracing.py)
- [agent.py](src/ark_agentic/agents/securities/agent.py)

</blog>
